questions.testName: December 2024 US Form C SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: A company studied a random sample of 20 lawns in a small town. The company found that 4 of these lawns contain fertilizer. If there are 260 lawns in the town, which of the following is the best estimate of the number of lawns that contain fertilizer?

questions.testName: December 2024 US Form C SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: The scatterplot shows 5 measurements of the body length, in centimeters (cm), of a New Zealand fur seal from an age of 1 year to 6 years old. A line of best fit is also shown. questions.testName: December 2024 US Form C SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: A group of 80 employees selected at random from all the employees at a company were surveyed about the number of books they read last year. From the data collected, it was estimated that the mean number of books employees at the company read last year is 7, with an associated margin of error of 1.6. Which of the following is the most appropriate conclusion?
questions.questionText: A group of 80 employees selected at random from all the employees at a company were surveyed about the number of books they read last year. From the data collected, it was estimated that the mean number of books employees at the company read last year is 7, with an associated margin of error of 1.6. Which of the following is the most appropriate conclusion?

choices.text: It is plausible that the actual mean number of books employees at the company read last year is less than 5.4.
It is plausible that the actual mean number of books employees at the company read last year is between 5.4 and 8.6.
It is plausible that every employee at the company read between 7 and 11.8 books last year.
It is plausible that the actual mean number of books employees at the company read last year is greater than 8.6.

category.subject: Math
category.skill: Inference from sample statistics and margin of error

questions.prompt: In triangle $DEF$, the measure of angle $D$ is 43° and the measure of angle $E$ is 112°. In triangle $RST$, the measure of angle $R$ is 43° and the measure of angle $S$ is 112°. Which of the following additional pieces of information is needed to determine whether triangle $DEF$ is similar to triangle $RST$?
questions.questionText: In triangle $DEF$, the measure of angle $D$ is 43° and the measure of angle $E$ is 112°. In triangle $RST$, the measure of angle $R$ is 43° and the measure of angle $S$ is 112°. choices.text: The measure of angle $F$ and the measure of angle $T$

questions.testName: December 2024 US Form C SAT Math Module 1

questions.testName: December 2024 US Form C SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: While the mass of an object is the same everywhere, the weight of an object is not the same on different planets. An object has a weight of 220.00 pounds on Earth and a weight of 234.52 pounds on Saturn. The object's weight on Jupiter is 252.8% of its weight on Earth. If the object's weight on Saturn is x% of its weight on Jupiter, which of the following is closest to the value of x?
questions.questionText: While the mass of an object is the same everywhere, the weight of an object is not the same on different planets. An object has a weight of 220.00 pounds on Earth and a weight of 234.52 pounds on Saturn. The object's weight on Jupiter is 252.8% of its weight on Earth. If the object's weight on Saturn is x% of its weight on Jupiter, which of the following is closest to the value of x?

questions.testName: December 2024 US Form A SAT Math Module 2

questions.testName: December 2024 US Form A SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt: The population of a certain city doubled every 25 years from 1660 to 1760. The population of this city was 208,000 in 1760. What was the population of this city in 1660?
questions.questionText: The population of a certain city doubled every 25 years from 1660 to 1760. The population of this city was 208,000 in 1760. What was the population of this city in 1660?

questions.testName: December 2024 US Form A SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt: The function $f(x)=600(0.5)^{\frac{x}{11}}$ gives the predicted intensity of a beam $f(x)$, in number of photons in the beam, $x$ millimeters below the surface of a certain material. What is the best interpretation of $f(11)= 300$?
questions.questionText: The function $f(x)=600(0.5)^{\frac{x}{11}}$ gives the predicted intensity of a beam $f(x)$, in number of photons in the beam, $x$ millimeters below the surface of a certain material. What is the best interpretation of $f(11)= 300$?

choices.text: A beam at the surface of the material has a predicted intensity of 11 photons in the beam.
A beam at the surface of the material has a predicted intensity of 300 photons in the beam.
A beam 11 millimeters below the surface of the material has a predicted intensity of 300 photons in the beam.
A beam 300 millimeters below the surface of the material has a predicted intensity of 600 photons in the beam.

questions.prompt: A carnival receives \$10 for each adult admission ticket sold and \$5 for each child admission ticket sold. If 220 adult admission tickets were sold on that day, how many child admission tickets were sold?

questions.testName: December 2024 US Form A SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt:
$c(r)=\frac{2}{5}(2\pi r)$
The function $c$ gives the partial length, in centimeters (cm), of the circumference of a circle with a radius that measures $r$ cm. According to the function, for each increase of 18 cm in the length of the radius, this partial length of the circumference increases by $k\pi$ cm. What is the value of $k$?

questions.testName: December 2024 US Form A SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt: A computer program models the population of a certain insect in an environment where the insect has no natural predators. According to the model, the estimated total mass of the population of insects at the end of every 5-week period is 158% greater than the estimated total mass of the population of insects at the end of the previous 5-week period. The estimated total mass of the population of insects at the end of 15 weeks is 627.7 grams. Which equation best represents this model, where $M$ is the estimated total mass, in grams, of the population of insects at the end of $t$ weeks?
questions.questionText: A computer program models the population of a certain insect in an environment where the insect has no natural predators. According to the model, the estimated total mass of the population of insects at the end of every 5-week period is 158% greater than the estimated total mass of the population of insects at the end of the previous 5-week period. The estimated total mass of the population of insects at the end of 15 weeks is 627.7 grams. Which equation best represents this model, where $M$ is the estimated total mass, in grams, of the population of insects at the end of $t$ weeks?

questions.testName: December 2024 US Form A SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt: The value of an autographed baseball increased by 178% from the end of 2012 to the end of 2013 and then decreased by 21% from the end of 2013 to the end of 2014. What was the net percentage increase in the value of the autographed baseball from the end of 2012 to the end of 2014?

questions.prompt: The function $f(x)=600(0.5)^{\frac{x}{11}}$ gives the predicted intensity of a beam $f(x)$, in number of photons in the beam, $x$ millimeters below the surface of a certain material. What is the best interpretation of $f(11)= 300$?
questions.questionText: The function $f(x)=600(0.5)^{\frac{x}{11}}$ gives the predicted intensity of a beam $f(x)$, in number of photons in the beam, $x$ millimeters below the surface of a certain material. What is the best interpretation of $f(11)= 300$?

choices.text: A beam at the surface of the material has a predicted intensity of 11 photons in the beam.
A beam at the surface of the material has a predicted intensity of 300 photons in the beam.
A beam 11 millimeters below the surface of the material has a predicted intensity of 300 photons in the beam.
A beam 300 millimeters below the surface of the material has a predicted intensity of 600 photons in the beam.

questions.testName: December 2024 US Form A SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt: A computer program models the population of a certain insect in an environment where the insect has no natural predators. According to the model, the estimated total mass of the population of insects at the end of every 5-week period is 158% greater than the estimated total mass of the population of insects at the end of the previous 5-week period. The estimated total mass of the population of insects at the end of 15 weeks is 627.7 grams. Which equation best represents this model, where $M$ is the estimated total mass, in grams, of the population of insects at the end of $t$ weeks?
questions.questionText: A computer program models the population of a certain insect in an environment where the insect has no natural predators. According to the model, the estimated total mass of the population of insects at the end of every 5-week period is 158% greater than the estimated total mass of the population of insects at the end of the previous 5-week period. The estimated total mass of the population of insects at the end of 15 weeks is 627.7 grams. Which equation best represents this model, where $M$ is the estimated total mass, in grams, of the population of insects at the end of $t$ weeks?
